The author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck, and they need to calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards dealt. This can be approached using combinatorial functions. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from the deck is $ \binom{52}{5} $. The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is $ \binom{36}{5} $, since there are 36 non-heart cards in the deck. The number of ways to get at least one heart is the total number of ways to choose 5 cards minus the number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts, which is $ \binom{52}{5} - \binom{36}{5} $. Therefore, the probability is $ 1 - \frac{\binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}} $.

***  

The author is faced with a decision between two strategies. Strategy A offers a 40% chance of winning $1000 and a 60% chance of losing $500, resulting in an expected value of $ 0.4 \times 1000 - 0.6 \times 500 = 400 - 300 = 100 $. Strategy B offers a 60% chance of winning $800 and a 40% chance of losing $200, resulting in an expected value of $ 0.6 \times 800 - 0.4 \times 200 = 480 - 80 = 400 $. Since Strategy B has a higher expected value, the author should choose Strategy B.  

Bagging the decision, the author should embrace the calculated risk and pursue Strategy B.
